findings.summary: The 2022 firm overview describes the follow-the-finance-lead pattern as the firm's established norm and credits Woods LLC with a readiness workstream, but the only instance of that pattern and the only readiness engagement both begin after the document's date, and Woods never had readiness work at all.
findings.evidence: d:0007 is dated 2022-04-30 and states: 'Representative work includes our engagement for Woods LLC, a relationship that has continued across valuation and readiness workstreams' and 'Most new work reaches us the same way: a finance lead we have served moves to a new company and brings the firm along. We consider that the only marketing worth having.' Per engagements.json, Woods LLC has exactly two engagements ever -- E-2020-001 Valuation Analysis and E-2023-001 Working Capital Assessment -- and no readiness work in any year. The firm's only Transaction Readiness Review is E-2022-001 for Nguyen, Reynolds and Martinez, starting 2022-05-28: 28 days AFTER this overview. The firm's only finance lead who moves employers is Amanda Golden, who leaves Woods for NRM on 2022-05-12 (foundation.json affiliations): 12 days AFTER this overview. On 2022-04-30 the pattern the overview calls 'the only marketing worth having' has happened zero times.

findings.summary: The NRM briefing deck calls itself a midpoint briefing and says the program is past its midpoint, contradicting the start date and duration printed in the same bullet list on the same slide; the deck lands at 25 percent of the engagement.
findings.evidence: d:0011 is dated 2022-07-18. Its 'Engagement Snapshot' list reads: 'Readiness review under way since {{fact:f:E-2022-001.start}}' (renders 'May 28, 2022') immediately followed by 'Seven-month program, now past its midpoint', and the header paragraph calls it a 'Midpoint client briefing prepared for Nguyen, Reynolds and Martinez'. E-2022-001 runs 2022-05-28 to 2022-12-18 (204 days); the deck sits 51 days in, 25 percent elapsed, and the true midpoint is 2022-09-07. A reader needs no other document: the slide states its own start date and length, and the file date refutes the midpoint claim. The manifest brief did not ask for a midpoint framing (title is only 'Briefing Deck: Transaction Readiness Review for Nguyen, Reynolds and Martinez'), and the parallel Woods deck d:0004, also placed at exactly 25 percent of its engagement, makes no fractional claim.

findings.summary: The same first-review milestone on the Woods valuation is reported complete, then back in progress, then complete again across four weeks, in three documents all reported by Sandra Perez.
findings.evidence: d:0003 (2020-07-10, Sandra's email): 'The income approach is drafted and through my first review.' d:0004 (2020-07-16, Sandra's deck), six days later: 'Income approach model drafted and in first review'. d:0005 (2020-08-06, minutes): 'Sandra Perez opened with model status. The income approach has cleared first review' -- announced as the period's news, four weeks after she first called it through review. The milestone oscillates cleared -> in progress -> cleared with no rework or reopening mentioned anywhere.

findings.summary: The Woods valuation ran June to November 2020 and carries no trace of the conditions of its own moment; recorded as an omission, not a contradiction, since nothing in the corpus states anything false.
findings.evidence: E-2020-001 is a fair-market-value analysis for board use running 2020-06-10 to 2020-11-01, using 'income and market approaches' and 'research into comparable companies and transactions' (d:0001). Across d:0002 through d:0006 no document mentions pandemic conditions, and d:0006's Risks section (2020-09-26) -- the natural place for it -- lists only 'Board calendar', 'Late data changes: any restatement of the monthly figures after factual review would require rework of the tie-out', and 'Availability: the review period overlaps client close activities'. The only period marker in the whole 2020 set is d:0005's 'held by video conference'. A valuation professional would expect a mid-2020 engagement to address the effect on comparables and normalization; its total absence reads as prose written without a date in mind. No document postdates itself or contradicts the ledger, so no defect is claimed.
